description_long: This object is a straight-edged sword with a long, rectangular blade that tapers to a pointed tip. The blade is uniformly dark in color, likely made of metal, and lacks any visible serrations or decorative features. A simple, slightly curved crossguard separates the blade from the handle, providing basic hand protection. The handle is cylindrical and appears to be made of the same material as the blade, ending in a rounded pommel for balance and grip security. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible ornamentation or embellishments. The sword's proportions suggest it is designed for two-handed use, typical of medieval longswords or training swords.
description: A straight, dark metal sword with a rectangular blade, simple crossguard, and cylindrical handle ending in a round pommel. The design is plain and functional, with no visible ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the straight blade, crossguard, and cylindrical handle with a round pommel.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin, flat profile of the blade and handle, with the crossguard appearing as a narrow line.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying the straight blade, crossguard, and handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile of the blade and handle, similar to view 1.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, straight, metal, rounded pommel, no visible grip texture
